stories.title: Add equals/hashCode to entity classes
stories.description: As a developer, I want proper equals() and hashCode() methods on entity classes so that equality semantics are consistent and collections work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Timesheet.java has equals() and hashCode() using id field
Course.java has equals() and hashCode() using id field
Approval.java has equals() and hashCode() using id field
TutorAssignment.java has equals() and hashCode() using id field
LecturerAssignment.java has equals() and hashCode() using id field
Handle null id (new entities) by checking identity
Use Objects.equals() and Objects.hash() for null-safety

stories.title: Externalize hardcoded strings to constants
stories.description: As a developer, I want hardcoded strings externalized so that magic strings are eliminated and typos are caught at compile time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create TimesheetStatus enum for status strings (DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED)
TimesheetRepository uses enum/constants instead of string literals
JwtAuthenticationFilter path strings moved to private static final Set<String> PUBLIC_PATHS

stories.title: Add null safety checks to UserApplicationService
stories.description: As a developer, I want null safety checks added so that NullPointerException risks are minimized and errors are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: Add null checks before .equals() calls using Objects.equals() or explicit null check
Use Objects.requireNonNull() for critical parameters in method entry points
Add @NonNull annotations on parameters where appropriate

stories.title: Add @Transactional(readOnly=true) to read methods
stories.description: As a developer, I want read-only service methods annotated so that database connection efficiency is improved and intent is clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @Transactional(readOnly=true) to methods that only read data
Do NOT add to methods that modify data (create, update, delete)

stories.title: Add Javadoc to remaining controllers
stories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive Javadoc on remaining controllers so that API behavior is fully documented.
acceptanceCriteria: TestDataResetController.java has class-level Javadoc noting it's for testing only
TestDataSeedController.java has class-level Javadoc noting it's for testing only

stories.title: Add Javadoc to entity classes
stories.description: As a developer, I want Javadoc on entity classes so that entity purpose and relationships are documented.
acceptanceCriteria: TutorAssignment.java has class-level Javadoc explaining the tutor-course relationship
TutorProfileDefaults.java has class-level Javadoc explaining default settings
Approval.java has class-level Javadoc explaining the approval workflow entity
LecturerAssignment.java has class-level Javadoc explaining lecturer-course relationship

stories.title: Add unit tests for ApprovalApplicationService
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want unit tests for ApprovalApplicationService so that approval workflow logic is verified in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test approve workflow (lecturer confirm, HR confirm)
Test error scenarios (invalid state transitions, unauthorized access)

stories.title: Add unit tests for TimesheetApplicationService
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want unit tests for TimesheetApplicationService so that timesheet business logic is verified in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
Test Schedule 1 calculation integration (verify calculator is called)
Test permission enforcement (user can only modify own timesheets)
Test validation errors (invalid hours, invalid status)

stories.title: Extend GlobalExceptionHandlerTest coverage
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want comprehensive tests for GlobalExceptionHandler so that all exception types are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify all responses use RFC-7807 ProblemDetail format
Verify traceId and timestamp are present in all responses

stories.title: Refactor Thread.sleep() to Clock injection
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want deterministic time-dependent tests so that tests pass consistently and quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: TimesheetRepositoryTest.java uses Clock.fixed() instead of Thread.sleep()
UserTest.java uses Clock.fixed() instead of Thread.sleep()
Use clock.instant().plusSeconds(n) to advance time instead of sleeping
Tests are deterministic and fast (no actual waiting)

stories.title: Create CONTRIBUTING.md
stories.description: As a new contributor, I want clear contribution guidelines so that I can follow project standards.
acceptanceCriteria: Include code style guidelines (Java conventions, formatting)
Include PR submission process (branch naming, commit messages)
Include testing requirements (unit tests, integration tests)
stories.verificationCommand: ls CONTRIBUTING.md && cat CONTRIBUTING.md | grep -q 'Code Style' && cat CONTRIBUTING.md | grep -q 'Pull Request'

stories.title: Add @DisplayName to tests missing them
stories.description: As a developer, I want descriptive @DisplayName annotations on tests so that test reports are readable in CI/CD.
acceptanceCriteria: Scan all test files in src/test/java/com/usyd/catams/
Add @DisplayName to test methods that only use method names
Display names should be human-readable sentences describing the test
Focus on recently modified or high-value test files first
Do not change tests that already have good @DisplayName
